facts.statement: Lam Research is a global supplier of wafer fabrication equipment and services to the semiconductor industry.
Lam Research was incorporated in 1980, is a Delaware corporation, and is headquartered in Fremont, California.
Lam Research's common stock trades on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under ticker LRCX.
The SEC submissions metadata identifies Lam Research's SIC description as "Special Industry Machinery, NEC."
Lam's customer base includes leading semiconductor memory, foundry, and integrated device manufacturers that make products such as non-volatile memory, DRAM, and logic devices.
Lam says its products and services help customers build devices used in mobile phones, personal computers, cloud and enterprise servers, wearables, automotive vehicles, and data storage devices.
Lam describes its core competencies as nanoscale manufacturing enablement, chemistry, plasma and fluidics, advanced systems engineering, and a broad range of operational disciplines.
Lam states that its core technical competency is integrating hardware, process, materials, software, and process control to enable results on the wafer.
Lam states that it has leadership and expertise in deposition, etch, and clean markets, which it believes position it to facilitate innovations in semiconductor device manufacturing.

Lam identifies several sources of potential sustainable differentiation: R&D focus, learning from its broad installed base, collaboration with semiconductor ecosystem partners, investment across its product portfolio for technology inflections, and delivery of multi-product solutions.
Lam Research reported FY2025 revenue of $17.428516 billion.
Lam Research reported FY2025 net income of $4.510931 billion.
Lam Research reported FY2025 operating cash flow of $5.178938 billion.
Lam Research reported FY2025 research and development expense of $1.727162 billion.
At FY2025, Lam Research reported $5.337056 billion of cash and equivalents, $6.278366 billion of stockholders' equity, and $4.466045 billion of noncurrent long-term debt.
Lam's FY2025 GAAP operating income was $5.900968 billion, equal to 32.0% of revenue; non-GAAP operating income was $5.957156 billion, equal to 32.3% of revenue.

Lam's inventories totaled $4.3 billion as of June 29, 2025, representing 20% of total assets, and Ernst & Young identified inventory valuation as a critical audit matter because excess and obsolete inventory estimates involve judgment affected by market and economic conditions outside the company's control.

Lam's 2025 10-K cautionary statement identifies risks and uncertainties including wafer fabrication equipment spending changes, quarterly results volatility, customer spending and demand, customer base composition, supply chain constraints and material costs, competition, and its ability to defend or gain market share.
Ernst & Young opined that Lam Research maintained effective internal control over financial reporting as of June 29, 2025, based on the COSO 2013 framework.
Ernst & Young stated that it has served as Lam Research's auditor since 1981.

Lam's 2025 10-K was signed by Timothy M. Archer as President, Chief Executive Officer and Director; Douglas R. Bettinger as Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer; and Christina C. Correia as Group Vice President and Chief Accounting Officer.
Abhijit Y. Talwalkar is identified as Chair of the Board in Lam's 2025 proxy materials and as Chairman in the 2025 10-K signature page.

Lam's compensation and human resources committee uses non-GAAP gross margin as a percentage of revenue and non-GAAP operating income as a percentage of revenue in assessing and making executive compensation determinations.
Lam has adopted a Corporate Code of Ethics that applies to all employees, officers, and directors, and the code is publicly available on the Investor Relations page of its website.
